title: Janicza Bravo and Brett Gelman Channel Rejection and Anxiety to Make ‘Lemon’ | The Big Picture (Ep. 22)
format: A warm-up on Janicza Bravo's Atlanta episode 'Juneteenth,' then Bravo and Brett Gelman on the five-year slog to make 'Lemon.'

segments.heading: Setting up Janicza Bravo: 'Juneteenth'
segments.note: with The Ringer's Allison Herman on the Atlanta episode Bravo directed

films.title: Eyes Wide Shut
films.note: Earn's in-episode description of the rich couple's party: 'a Spike Lee-directed reboot of Eyes Wide Shut.'

films.title: Get Out
films.note: Herman's other reference point for 'Juneteenth': white affluence weaponized into dread, though less visceral here.

segments.heading: In conversation
segments.guest: Janicza Bravo and Brett Gelman on

films.title: Lemon
films.note: The surreal, absurd comedy of failure the couple spent five years making, from a first draft banged out in five days at SXSW off a lie about having a feature. Gelman plays Isaac; Bravo pitches it as 'come watch me fail for 90 minutes,' a rebuke to the loser-white-guy comedy where everything works out.

films.title: Eat
films.note: Bravo's first short, starring Gelman and Katherine Waterston; it played SXSW and started the run that led to Lemon.

films.title: Gregory Go Boom
films.note: The short Bravo wrote for Jash after Lemon's financing collapsed and its lead actor dropped out — her follow-up to a year lost to depression.
